_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in deploying, configuring, and scaling data platform components using infrastructure-as-code with version control and automated testing.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI DA domain. Skill DA-S-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-009 (SP, cosine: 0.436), SP-S-008 (SP, cosine: 0.410).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.5/10 (raw 0.654). Shared vocabulary: [deploying, skill]. NICE-only terms [securely, software] vs WIDAI-only terms [automated, code, components, configuring, control, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.890 (P=0.916, R=0.865). P exceeds R by 0.050 — the NICE element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 17 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Skill in deploying software securely" and "Skill in deploying, configuring, and scaling data platform components using infr" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.461 < STS 0.654 (gap -0.193). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [securely, software] and [automated, code, components] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.0%. Shared tokens: [deploying, skill]. NICE-only: [securely, software]. WIDAI-only: [automated, code, components, configuring, control, data].
